# Tasks: Configurable Market Feed Type
**Issue:** #112
**Use case:** `view-profile` (Platform Settings tab extension) + `update-user-settings`
**Generated by:** decomposer-agent — 2026-07-23

---

## Layer: DB — Database

### [DB-1] — Create UserSettings JPA entity

**Layer:** Database
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** view-profile / update-user-settings
**Implements:** `domain/model/user-settings.md` — full entity definition
**Inputs:**
- `domain/model/user-settings.md` — all field definitions, constraints, business rules

**Outputs:**
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.model.UserSettings` — JPA entity class
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.model.FeedType` — Kotlin enum (`SYNTHETIC`, `REAL`)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `UserSettings` is a plain `class` (not `data class`) annotated `@Entity @Table(name = "user_settings")`.
- [ ] Implements `Persistable<UUID>` with `@Transient private val _isNew: Boolean = true`.
- [ ] Fields: `id: UUID` (`@Id`, `@Column(nullable = false, updatable = false)`), `userId: UUID` (`@Column(nullable = false, unique = true, updatable = false)`), `feedType: FeedType` (`@Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)`, `@Column(nullable = false)`), `updatedAt: Instant` (`@UpdateTimestamp`).
- [ ] `FeedType` enum declares `SYNTHETIC` and `REAL` values.
- [ ] `equals` and `hashCode` implemented based on `id` only.
- [ ] `toString` implemented manually.
- [ ] No `@GeneratedValue` — `id` is pre-assigned by the service layer.
- [ ] No `createdAt` field — not present in the domain model.

**Depends on:** none

---

### [DB-2] — Extend User JPA entity with UserSettings one-to-one relationship

**Layer:** Database
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** view-profile / update-user-settings
**Implements:** `domain/model/user.md` — UserSettings one-to-one relationship
**Inputs:**
- Existing `org.dpp.tradelab.user.model.User` entity
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.model.UserSettings` (from DB-1)

**Outputs:**
- Updated `org.dpp.tradelab.user.model.User` with `@OneToOne(mappedBy = "userId", cascade = [CascadeType.ALL], fetch = FetchType.LAZY, optional = false)` relationship to `UserSettings`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `User` entity has a `settings: UserSettings` field annotated `@OneToOne(mappedBy = "userId", cascade = [CascadeType.ALL], fetch = FetchType.LAZY, optional = false)`.
- [ ] No new column is added to the `users` table — the FK lives on `user_settings.user_id`.
- [ ] All existing `User` fields and constraints remain unchanged.

**Depends on:** DB-1

**Acceptance criteria:**

- [ ] Interface declares `findAllBy(): List<UserSettings>` (used at Market Data startup to seed the feed-type cache).
- [ ] No business logic — pure Spring Data interface.

## Layer: SVC — Service

### [SVC-1] — Extend UserService to create default UserSettings at registration

**Layer:** Service
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** register-user (extension)
**Implements:** `domain/flows/user-registration.md` — step 9 (Create default UserSettings)
**Inputs:**
- `userId: UUID` — the newly created user's ID
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.repository.UserSettingsRepository` (from REPO-1)
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.model.FeedType.SYNTHETIC` — default value

**Outputs:**
- Persisted `UserSettings` row with `feedType = SYNTHETIC`, created atomically within the existing `@Transactional` registration method in `UserService`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] The existing `registerUser` service method (already `@Transactional`) creates a `UserSettings` instance with `id = UUID.randomUUID()`, `userId` = new user's id, `feedType = FeedType.SYNTHETIC`, and calls `userSettingsRepository.save(settings)`.
- [ ] Creation happens inside the same transaction as the `User` record — if either write fails, both roll back.
- [ ] No `UserSettingsChangedEvent` is emitted at registration — only at explicit PATCH updates.
- [ ] Unit test: `registerUser_validRequest_createsDefaultUserSettings` — asserts `userSettingsRepository.save` is called with the correct defaults.

**Depends on:** DB-1, DB-2, REPO-1

---

### [SVC-2] — Extend UserService to return settings inline with user profile

**Layer:** Service
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** view-profile
**Implements:** `domain/flows/user-session.md` Flow B — user profile fetch; `domain/model/user-settings.md` — "returned inline with user profile"
**Inputs:**
- `userId: UUID`
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.repository.UserRepository` (existing)
- `UserSettings` loaded via the `User → settings` one-to-one relationship (from DB-2)

**Outputs:**
- Updated `getUserById` service method response to include `settings` data alongside user profile fields

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] The existing `getUserById` (or equivalent) service method fetches the `User` entity including its `settings` via the one-to-one relationship (eager join or explicit load — whichever avoids N+1).
- [ ] The returned value exposes `feedType` and `updatedAt` from the associated `UserSettings`.
- [ ] Unit test: `getUserById_existingUser_returnsSettingsInline` — asserts that settings fields are present in the returned result.

**Depends on:** DB-2, REPO-1

### [SVC-3] — Implement updateUserSettings in UserService

**Layer:** Service
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** update-user-settings
**Implements:** `domain/flows/update-user-settings.md` — steps 8–10
**Inputs:**
- `userId: UUID` — from authenticated session (path param)
- `feedType: FeedType?` — nullable; only update if present
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.repository.UserSettingsRepository` (from REPO-1)
- `ApplicationEventPublisher`
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.exception.UserSettingsNotFoundException` (from EXCEPTION-1)

**Outputs:**
- Updated `UserSettings` row persisted
- `UserSettingsChangedEvent` published
- Returns updated `UserSettings` to the caller

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Method is annotated `@Transactional`.
- [ ] Loads `UserSettings` by `userId`; throws `UserSettingsNotFoundException` if not found.
- [ ] If `feedType` is non-null, updates `settings.feedType` to the supplied value.
- [ ] Calls `userSettingsRepository.save(settings)` — `updatedAt` is refreshed by `@UpdateTimestamp`.
- [ ] Publishes `UserSettingsChangedEvent(userId, feedType = settings.feedType, updatedAt = settings.updatedAt)` via `ApplicationEventPublisher`.
- [ ] Unit test: `updateUserSettings_validFeedType_persistsAndEmitsEvent` — asserts save called and event published with correct payload.
- [ ] Unit test: `updateUserSettings_settingsNotFound_throwsUserSettingsNotFoundException`.
- [ ] Unit test: `updateUserSettings_sameValue_stillPersistsAndEmitsEvent` — no-op case still saves and emits.

**Depends on:** REPO-1, EXCEPTION-1, EVT-1

**Acceptance criteria:**

- [ ] No Spring annotations on the event class itself.

**Depends on:** DB-1

---

### [EVT-2] — Create UserEventListener in user.messaging

**Layer:** Event
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** update-user-settings (internal — no cross-domain listener needed in User domain itself)
**Implements:** `standards/backend.md` — Listener class pattern (mandatory); ensures pattern compliance even if no intra-domain listener is needed
**Note:** The User domain does not consume its own `UserSettingsChangedEvent` internally. This task creates the listener class shell in the correct package so it is ready for any future intra-domain event consumption. The Market Data listener is a separate task (EVT-3).
**Inputs:** none
**Outputs:**
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.messaging.UserEventListener` — `@Component` listener class (empty or with any future intra-domain listeners)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Class is annotated `@Component`.
- [ ] Follows the listener class pattern: constructor-injected service, no business logic in listener methods.
- [ ] Unit test file exists (even if only a placeholder asserting the class instantiates correctly).

**Depends on:** EVT-1

---

### [EVT-3] — Create UserEventListener in marketdata.messaging to handle UserSettingsChangedEvent

**Layer:** Event
**Domain:** marketdata
**Use case:** update-user-settings (Market Data reaction)
**Implements:** `domain/flows/market-data-feed-routing.md` — Flow B (Update Feed-Type Cache on Settings Change)
**Inputs:**
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.messaging.UserSettingsChangedEvent` (from EVT-1)
- `MarketDataFeedService` (existing service — extended in SVC-4)

**Outputs:**
- `org.dpp.tradelab.marketdata.messaging.UserEventListener` — `@Component` listener class with `@EventListener fun onUserSettingsChanged(event: UserSettingsChangedEvent)`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Class is annotated `@Component`, named `UserEventListener`, lives in `marketdata.messaging`.
- [ ] Has a single listener method `onUserSettingsChanged(event: UserSettingsChangedEvent)` annotated `@EventListener`.
- [ ] Listener method body is exactly: `marketDataFeedService.handleUserSettingsChanged(event)`.
- [ ] No business logic inside the listener method.
- [ ] Unit test: `onUserSettingsChanged_event_delegatesToService` — mocks `MarketDataFeedService`, asserts `handleUserSettingsChanged` is called with the event.

**Depends on:** EVT-1, SVC-4

---

## Layer: SVC — Service (Market Data domain)

### [SVC-4] — Extend MarketDataFeedService with feed-type cache and handleUserSettingsChanged

**Layer:** Service
**Domain:** marketdata
**Use case:** update-user-settings (Market Data side)
**Implements:** `domain/flows/market-data-feed-routing.md` — Flow A (seed cache), Flow B (update cache on event), Flow C (route per user on tick), Flow D (apply at connection time)
**Inputs:**
- `UserSettingsChangedEvent` (from EVT-1)
- `UserSettingsApi` — User domain cross-domain interface (from API-1 below) — used to bulk-read all settings at startup
- Existing tick dispatch logic in `MarketDataFeedService`

**Outputs:**
- In-memory `feedTypeCache: ConcurrentHashMap<UUID, FeedType>` on `MarketDataFeedService`
- `seedFeedTypeCache()` method — called at startup via `@PostConstruct` or `ApplicationReadyEvent` listener
- `handleUserSettingsChanged(event: UserSettingsChangedEvent)` — overwrites cache entry
- Updated tick dispatch and snapshot methods to consult the cache (defaulting to `SYNTHETIC` on cache miss, logging a warning)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `feedTypeCache` is a `ConcurrentHashMap<UUID, FeedType>` field on the service.
- [ ] `seedFeedTypeCache()` calls `userSettingsApi.getAllUserSettings()` and populates the cache with `userId → feedType` for every returned row. Called at startup before any WebSocket connections are accepted.
- [ ] `handleUserSettingsChanged(event)` overwrites `feedTypeCache[event.userId]` with `event.feedType`.
- [ ] Snapshot dispatch (Flow A) reads `feedTypeCache[userId] ?: FeedType.SYNTHETIC` before building data. In this iteration both values resolve to synthetic.
- [ ] Tick dispatch (Flow B) reads `feedTypeCache[userId] ?: FeedType.SYNTHETIC` per connected subscriber. Cache miss logs a WARN and falls back to `SYNTHETIC`.
- [ ] Unit test: `handleUserSettingsChanged_updatesCache` — verifies cache entry is overwritten.
- [ ] Unit test: `seedFeedTypeCache_populatesCacheFromApi` — verifies all entries seeded from `userSettingsApi`.
- [ ] Unit test: `dispatchTick_cacheMiss_fallsBackToSynthetic`.

**Depends on:** EVT-1, DB-1, API-1

---

## Layer: API — Cross-Domain Interface

### [API-1] — Create UserSettingsApi interface in user.api

**Layer:** Service (API sub-layer)
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** update-user-settings (Market Data startup seed)
**Implements:** `domain/flows/market-data-feed-routing.md` — Flow A step 1 (read all UserSettings at startup); `standards/architecture.md` — Sync cross-domain communication via `api/` interfaces
**Inputs:**
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.repository.UserSettingsRepository` (from REPO-1)
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.model.UserSettings` (from DB-1)

**Outputs:**
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.api.UserSettingsApi` — Kotlin interface
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.api.UserSettingsApiImpl` — implementation class in `user.api` or `user.service`, injected into `UserSettingsApi`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `UserSettingsApi` declares `getAllUserSettings(): List<UserSettingsDto>` where `UserSettingsDto` is a simple data class `(userId: UUID, feedType: FeedType)` defined in `user.api` (not a JPA entity).
- [ ] `UserSettingsApiImpl` implements the interface by calling `userSettingsRepository.findAllBy()` and mapping each row to `UserSettingsDto`.
- [ ] The Market Data domain imports only `user.api.UserSettingsApi` and `user.api.UserSettingsDto` — never from `user.model`, `user.repository`, or `user.service`.
- [ ] Unit test: `getAllUserSettings_returnsAllRows` — mocks repository, asserts correct mapping.

**Depends on:** REPO-1, DB-1

## Layer: CONTROLLER — Controller

### [CONTROLLER-1] — Implement PATCH /users/{userId}/settings delegate method

**Layer:** Controller
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** update-user-settings
**Implements:** `domain/flows/update-user-settings.md` — steps 5–11; `standards/backend.md` — delegate pattern
**Inputs:**
- Generated `UserApiDelegate` (from API-CONTRACT-1)
- `userId: UUID` — from path parameter
- `UpdateUserSettingsRequest` — generated DTO (from API-CONTRACT-1); contains `feedType: String?`
- `UserService.updateUserSettings(userId, feedType)` (from SVC-3)

**Outputs:**
- `org.dpp.tradelab.user.controller.UserApiDelegateImpl` — updated to implement the new `updateUserSettings` delegate method
- Returns `ResponseEntity<UserSettingsResponse>` (HTTP 200) with the updated settings object

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `updateUserSettings(userId, updateUserSettingsRequest)` method implemented in `UserApiDelegateImpl`.
- [ ] Converts `updateUserSettingsRequest.feedType` (String) to `FeedType` enum; throws `InvalidFeedTypeException` (from EXCEPTION-2) if the value is not a valid enum constant.
- [ ] Calls `userService.updateUserSettings(userId, feedType)`.
- [ ] Maps the returned `UserSettings` to `UserSettingsResponse` and returns `ResponseEntity.ok(response)`.
- [ ] `@SpringBootTest` + MockMvc test: `updateUserSettings_validRequest_returns200WithSettings`.
- [ ] `@SpringBootTest` + MockMvc test: `updateUserSettings_invalidFeedType_returns400`.
- [ ] `@SpringBootTest` + MockMvc test: `updateUserSettings_settingsNotFound_returns404`.

**Depends on:** SVC-3, EXCEPTION-2, API-CONTRACT-1

## Layer: API-CONTRACT — OpenAPI Contract

### [API-CONTRACT-1] — Update user-openapi.yaml with PATCH settings endpoint and settings schema

**Layer:** API-CONTRACT
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** update-user-settings / view-profile
**Implements:** `domain/flows/update-user-settings.md` — full HTTP surface; `domain/model/user-settings.md` — all fields; `standards/backend.md` — OpenAPI contract conventions
**Inputs:**
- New path: `PATCH /users/{userId}/settings`
- New request schema: `UpdateUserSettingsRequest` — `{ feedType?: string (enum: SYNTHETIC, REAL) }`
- New response schema: `UserSettingsResponse` — `{ feedType: string, updatedAt: string (date-time) }`
- Updated schema: `UserResponse` — extended with required nested `settings: UserSettingsResponse`
- Error responses: 400, 401, 403, 404

**Outputs:**
- Updated `services/contract/user-openapi.yaml`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `PATCH /users/{userId}/settings` operation added with `operationId: updateUserSettings`.
- [ ] Request body: `UpdateUserSettingsRequest` with optional `feedType` field (`type: string`, `enum: [SYNTHETIC, REAL]`).
- [ ] Response 200: `UserSettingsResponse` with required `feedType` (string enum) and `updatedAt` (date-time).
- [ ] Response 400: `ErrorResponse` — invalid feedType value.
- [ ] Response 401: `ErrorResponse` — unauthenticated.
- [ ] Response 403: `ErrorResponse` — path userId does not match session user.
- [ ] Response 404: `ErrorResponse` — settings not found.
- [ ] `UserResponse` schema extended with a required `settings` property of type `UserSettingsResponse`.
- [ ] `UserSettingsResponse` schema defined under `components/schemas`.
- [ ] `UpdateUserSettingsRequest` schema defined under `components/schemas`.
- [ ] Existing paths and schemas are unchanged — only additive modifications.
- [ ] `info.title` remains `Trade Lab API — User`.

**Depends on:** none (contract is written first; implementation tasks reference it)

- [ ] Response is typed as `UserSettingsResponse { feedType: 'SYNTHETIC' | 'REAL'; updatedAt: string }`.

- [ ] `updateSettings` action replaces `session.settings` with the supplied object.

- [ ] Unit test: `clearSession - sets settings to null`.

### [COMP-1] — Create FeedTypeSelector component

**Layer:** Component
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** update-user-settings
**Implements:** `domain/flows/update-user-settings.md` — steps 2–3 (dropdown with tooltips); `domain/usecases/view-profile.md` — Platform Settings tab step 3–4
**Inputs:**
- `currentFeedType: 'SYNTHETIC' | 'REAL'`
- `onFeedTypeChange: (feedType: 'SYNTHETIC' | 'REAL') => void`
- `isPending: boolean` — controls loading/disabled state on the dropdown
- `isError: boolean` — controls inline error state

**Outputs:**
- `src/domains/user/components/FeedTypeSelector.tsx`
- `FeedTypeSelectorProps` TypeScript interface

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Renders a labelled dropdown (`<select>` or equivalent) with options "Synthetic Feed" and "Real Market Feed".
- [ ] Each option has an accessible tooltip (e.g. `title` attribute or custom tooltip component) with the text from the use case doc.
- [ ] Dropdown is disabled while `isPending` is true; shows a loading indicator on the dropdown.
- [ ] When `isError` is true, shows an inline error message: "Failed to save. Please try again."
- [ ] Calls `onFeedTypeChange` with the new value when the user selects an option.
- [ ] Unit test: `FeedTypeSelector - renders current value selected`.
- [ ] Unit test: `FeedTypeSelector - calls onFeedTypeChange on selection`.
- [ ] Unit test: `FeedTypeSelector - is disabled when isPending is true`.
- [ ] Unit test: `FeedTypeSelector - shows error message when isError is true`.

**Depends on:** STATE-2

**Depends on:** COMP-1, STATE-2

### [SCREEN-1] — Extend ProfilePage to add Platform Settings tab

**Layer:** Screen
**Domain:** user
**Use case:** view-profile
**Implements:** `domain/usecases/view-profile.md` — full updated use case; `domain/flows/update-user-settings.md` — step 1 (navigate to tab)
**Inputs:**
- Existing `ProfilePage` in `src/domains/user/pages/ProfilePage.tsx`
- `PlatformSettingsSection` component (from COMP-2)
- `session.settings` from Zustand store

**Outputs:**
- Updated `ProfilePage` with tabbed layout: "Profile Information" (existing content) and "Platform Settings" (new tab rendering `PlatformSettingsSection`)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Profile page renders two tabs: "Profile Information" and "Platform Settings".
- [ ] "Profile Information" tab content is unchanged from the current implementation.
- [ ] "Platform Settings" tab renders `PlatformSettingsSection`.
- [ ] If no active session, page redirects to `/login` (existing behaviour, unchanged).
- [ ] Unit test: `ProfilePage - renders Profile Information tab by default`.
- [ ] Unit test: `ProfilePage - renders Platform Settings tab when selected`.
- [ ] Unit test: `ProfilePage - redirects to login when no session`.

**Depends on:** COMP-2
